description: Working as a Research Extern on an AI-driven project that leverages large language models and knowledge graphs to extract and reason over structured insights from unstructured automotive research data.

Joined the Cloud Identity Engineering team to harden container security across a large-scale Kubernetes environment, working at the intersection of DevOps and cloud security to automate vulnerability detection and reporting across 90+ microservices.
achievements: Built a Python script to query Kubernetes namespaces, interface with the Harbor API, and run Trivy vulnerability scans — automating security checks across the container ecosystem.
Orchestrated a Jenkins CI/CD pipeline for 90+ Dockerized microservices to run weekly security scans and publish buildability and vulnerability reports directly to team Slack channels.
Cut critical production vulnerabilities by 25%, eliminated 100% of stale images, and saved the team 5 hours/week of manual monitoring — adopted as the new SDLC standard.

description: Played a pivotal role in developing a greenfield application for the bank's Reconciliations Department, targeting projected savings of EUR 45M over 5 years while consolidating 14 legacy systems.
achievements: Consolidated 14 legacy reconciliation apps into a single strategic platform using Agile development, contributing to EUR 45M in long-term cost savings.
Built analyst-facing config utilities in React and JavaScript that streamlined the promotion process and cut manual errors by 60%.
Optimised a high-throughput reconciliation matching workflow using Apache Spark and SQL in Spring Boot, boosting processing throughput by 40%.
Engineered a Java-based interpreter to translate analyst-authored JSON configs into executable runtime logic, eliminating developer bottlenecks for 50+ reconciliations.
Improved production reliability using checkpointing and feature flags in Spring Boot microservices via REST APIs and ActiveMQ, enabling retry/resume and reducing failure recovery time.
Strengthened platform security by redesigning JWT-based auth and implementing a segmented Oracle DB architecture with service-specific users to minimise cross-service data access.
Raised React.js test coverage to 80%+ with Jest and React Testing Library, and established frontend best practices adopted across the team.

description: During my internship at Altruist Technologies, I independently developed a secure virtual hospital web application as a separate project. This experience allowed me to grasp the end-to-end development of a web application.
achievements: Architected & developed a secure Virtual Hospital web application using SpringBoot and native HTML/CSS with key functionalities like appointment booking, prescription generation, notification workflows & a document repository.
Engineered a robust WebRTC framework using WebSocket for real-time collaboration capabilities.

description: Gained foundational industry experience contributing to web development projects, building core skills in server-side scripting and frontend development while working within a professional engineering team for the first time.
